title: oplismenus hispidus
intro: Oplismenus hispidus, also known as hairy false millet grass, is a type of grass native to Australia. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Oplismenus hispidus include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water.
